summary:
Fundal pressure, McRoberts' maneuver, and suprapubic pressure are primary actions taken during initial response; whereas the Woods maneuver may only come into play if these fail due its nature being more complex/advanced intervention rather than a standard part of routine care steps. Agent2 concludes by emphasizing the distinction between common early-stage procedures versus specialized later-stage techniques like the Woods maneuver when other methods have not resolved issues promptly enough. This summary highlights critical differences among various strategies used in managing shoulder dystocia based primarily around their immediacy and complexity levels required within different scenarios encountered clinically.
